description.high: A task management web application with a REST API, persistent storage, and email notifications for due tasks — deployed as AWS Lambda functions.
description.tech: AWS Lambda handlers routed via API Gateway proxy events; repository pattern over DynamoDB via boto3; EventBridge Scheduler triggers overdue-task notifications via Amazon SES.

description.high: The filing cabinet. Saves tasks to DynamoDB so they survive Lambda cold starts, and finds them again on request.
description.tech: Repository pattern over AWS DynamoDB via boto3. Maps Task dataclasses to DynamoDB items; UUID-based partition keys; optional FilterExpression scans for status filtering. Table is provisioned externally (Terraform).

blocks.name: TaskRepository

description.high: Saves tasks to DynamoDB and retrieves them, with optional filtering by status.
description.tech: CRUD over a DynamoDB table. __init__ accepts a table_name string (default from DYNAMODB_TABLE env var, fallback 'tasks') and creates a boto3.resource('dynamodb').Table reference. No _migrate() — table is provisioned by Terraform. save(task) calls table.put_item, assigning a uuid4().hex id if task.id is None and storing due_date as ISO string. find(status) calls table.scan with an optional FilterExpression=Attr('status').eq(status) from boto3.dynamodb.conditions, then hydrates each item dict via Task.from_row.

inputs.name: table_name
inputs.description: DynamoDB table name; defaults to DYNAMODB_TABLE env var or 'tasks'

inputs.name: task
inputs.description: Task dataclass instance passed to save()

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Optional status filter string passed to find(); None returns all tasks

outputs.name: saved_task
outputs.description: Task returned by save() with id assigned from uuid4().hex

outputs.name: tasks
outputs.description: List of hydrated Task dataclasses returned by find()

blocks.name: Task

description.high: The shape of a single task: its id, title, due date, priority level, and whether it's done.
description.tech: Dataclass with fields id (str|None), title (str), due_date (datetime), priority (int, default 2), status (str, default 'pending'). Provides a from_row(row) classmethod that hydrates an instance from either a plain dict or any mapping (e.g. sqlite3.Row) using datetime.fromisoformat for due_date. The id field is str (UUID hex) to match DynamoDB usage.

inputs.name: row
inputs.description: Plain dict or mapping (e.g. DynamoDB item) passed to from_row(); must contain id, title, due_date, priority, status keys

outputs.name: task
outputs.description: Hydrated Task instance returned by from_row()

description.high: Sends you an email listing every overdue task so nothing slips through the cracks — delivered via Amazon SES.
description.tech: EmailNotifier reads AWS_SES_REGION and SES_FROM_EMAIL from environment variables. send_overdue_digest(overdue, to) renders a plaintext digest of overdue Task objects and delivers it via boto3 SES send_email. Returns 0 if the list is empty, 1 after a successful send.

blocks.name: EmailNotifier

description.high: Composes and sends a single digest email via Amazon SES listing every overdue task. Does nothing if there are no overdue tasks.
description.tech: Reads AWS_SES_REGION (default 'us-east-1') and SES_FROM_EMAIL from os.environ in __init__. Creates self.ses = boto3.client('ses', region_name=self.region). send_overdue_digest(overdue: list[Task], to: str) short-circuits and returns 0 when overdue is empty; otherwise builds a plaintext body from task.title and task.due_date, then calls self.ses.send_email with Source=self.from_email, Destination={'ToAddresses': [to]}, and a Message dict containing Subject and Body. Returns 1 on success.

inputs.name: overdue
inputs.description: List of Task instances that are past their due_date; passed to send_overdue_digest()

inputs.name: to
inputs.description: Recipient email address passed to send_overdue_digest()

outputs.name: sent
outputs.description: 0 if overdue list was empty and no email was sent; 1 after a successful SES send

description.high: The front door. Handles incoming HTTP requests from API Gateway and scheduled EventBridge events to manage tasks and send overdue notifications.
description.tech: Two AWS Lambda handler functions. lambda_handler routes API Gateway proxy events: GET /tasks calls TaskRepository.find() and returns a JSON list; POST /tasks parses the body, constructs a Task, calls TaskRepository.save(), and returns the saved task as JSON. notify_handler is triggered by EventBridge Scheduler: calls TaskRepository.find(status='pending'), filters tasks where due_date < datetime.utcnow(), and calls EmailNotifier.send_overdue_digest().

blocks.name: lambda_handler

description.high: Receives HTTP requests from API Gateway and routes them to the right task operation — listing tasks or creating a new one.
description.tech: AWS Lambda handler function receiving an API Gateway proxy event dict and Lambda context. Routes on event['httpMethod'] and event['path']. GET /tasks: extracts optional 'status' from queryStringParameters, calls TaskRepository.find(status), serializes results to JSON (converting datetime to isoformat and handling Decimal for DynamoDB numeric types). POST /tasks: parses event['body'] as JSON, constructs Task(id=None, title=..., due_date=datetime.fromisoformat(...), priority=..., status=...), calls TaskRepository.save(task), returns saved task as JSON. All responses are API Gateway proxy response dicts with statusCode, headers (Content-Type: application/json), and a JSON-serialized body. Returns 405 for unsupported methods and 500 with error message on exceptions.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: API Gateway proxy event dict containing httpMethod, path, queryStringParameters, and body

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: AWS Lambda context object

outputs.name: response
outputs.description: API Gateway proxy response dict with statusCode (int), headers (dict), and body (JSON string)

blocks.name: notify_handler

description.high: Runs on a schedule to find all pending tasks that are past due and emails a digest to the configured recipient.
description.tech: AWS Lambda handler triggered by EventBridge Scheduler. Calls TaskRepository.find(status='pending') to retrieve all pending tasks, then filters to those where task.due_date < datetime.utcnow() to identify overdue tasks. Passes the overdue list to EmailNotifier.send_overdue_digest(overdue, os.environ['NOTIFY_EMAIL']). Returns a summary dict with the count of overdue tasks and the SES send result. Raises KeyError if NOTIFY_EMAIL env var is not set.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: EventBridge Scheduler event dict (contents not used)

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: AWS Lambda context object

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Summary dict with 'overdue_count' (int) and 'sent' (0 or 1) keys

blocks.code:
import os
from datetime import datetime

def notify_handler(event: dict, context) -> dict:
    """EventBridge-triggered handler that sends overdue task digest via SES."""
    repo = TaskRepository()
    notifier = EmailNotifier()
    to_email = os.environ['NOTIFY_EMAIL']

    pending = repo.find(status='pending')
    now = datetime.utcnow()
    overdue = [t for t in pending if t.due_date.replace(tzinfo=None) < now]

    sent = notifier.send_overdue_digest(overdue, to_email)
    return {'overdue_count': len(overdue), 'sent': sent}

description.high: All the AWS plumbing declared as code — DynamoDB table, two Lambda functions, an HTTP API via API Gateway, an EventBridge schedule for daily digests, SES sender verification, and a remote Terraform state backend with locking.
description.tech: Terraform HCL configuration targeting the AWS provider. Declares aws_dynamodb_table (PAY_PER_REQUEST, GSI on status), two aws_lambda_function resources sharing one IAM role with least-privilege inline policies (DynamoDB PutItem/Scan + SES SendEmail), aws_apigatewayv2_api HTTP API with two routes and Lambda integrations, aws_scheduler_schedule for the notify Lambda (daily 08:00 UTC), aws_ses_email_identity for sender verification, input variables with sensitive flags, and an S3+DynamoDB remote backend.

blocks.name: variables.tf

description.high: Declares all tuneable inputs for the deployment: AWS region, DynamoDB table name, the sender email address, and the notification recipient. Sensitive values must be supplied at plan/apply time and must never be committed to source control.
description.tech: Terraform variable blocks. `aws_region` (string, default 'us-east-1') drives the provider and SES client region. `table_name` (string, default 'tasks') is passed to aws_dynamodb_table and injected into Lambda env as DYNAMODB_TABLE. `ses_from_email` (string, sensitive=true, no default) is used for aws_ses_email_identity and the SES_FROM_EMAIL Lambda env var. `notify_email` (string, sensitive=true, no default) is injected as NOTIFY_EMAIL. A terraform.tfvars.example comment documents the expected shape without revealing real values.

outputs.name: aws_region
outputs.description: AWS region for all resources; default us-east-1

outputs.name: table_name
outputs.description: DynamoDB table name; default tasks

outputs.name: ses_from_email
outputs.description: SES verified sender address; sensitive, no default

outputs.name: notify_email
outputs.description: Digest recipient address; sensitive, no default

blocks.code:
# variables.tf
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# All tuneable inputs for the TaskFlow deployment.
# Sensitive vars have no defaults — supply via terraform.tfvars
# (gitignored) or via TF_VAR_* environment variables in CI.
#
# terraform.tfvars.example (copy → terraform.tfvars, DO NOT COMMIT):
#   aws_region     = "us-east-1"
#   table_name     = "tasks"
#   ses_from_email = "sender@example.com"
#   notify_email   = "ops@example.com"
# ---------------------------------------------------------------

variable "aws_region" {
  description = "AWS region for all resources."
  type        = string
  default     = "us-east-1"
}

variable "table_name" {
  description = "Name of the DynamoDB tasks table."
  type        = string
  default     = "tasks"
}

variable "ses_from_email" {
  description = "SES-verified sender email address used by the notification Lambda."
  type        = string
  sensitive   = true
}

variable "notify_email" {
  description = "Recipient email address for the daily overdue-task digest."
  type        = string
  sensitive   = true
}

blocks.name: backend.tf

description.high: Tells Terraform to store its state file safely in an S3 bucket with encryption, and use a DynamoDB table to prevent two people applying changes at the same time.
description.tech: Terraform S3 backend block with encrypt=true. Placeholders must be replaced with a pre-existing S3 bucket (versioning enabled, public access blocked) and a DynamoDB table with a LockID (String) hash key for state locking. The state key is 'taskflow/terraform.tfstate'. Provider block pins the AWS provider version to ~> 5.0 and reads the region from var.aws_region.

blocks.name: aws_dynamodb_table.tasks

description.high: Creates the DynamoDB table that stores all tasks. Uses on-demand billing so you only pay per request, enables automatic backups so you can restore to any point in the last 35 days, and encrypts all data at rest.
description.tech: aws_dynamodb_table resource. billing_mode=PAY_PER_REQUEST eliminates capacity planning. Hash key 'id' (S) is the UUID hex primary key used by TaskRepository. A global secondary index 'status-index' on attribute 'status' (S) with KEYS_ONLY projection supports TaskRepository.find(status=...) FilterExpression scans without reading full items. point_in_time_recovery enabled. server_side_encryption uses AWS managed key (aws_owned_cmk=false defaults to AWS-managed CMK). Table name sourced from var.table_name.

inputs.name: var.table_name
inputs.description: DynamoDB table name, default 'tasks'

outputs.name: aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.name
outputs.description: Actual table name, referenced by Lambda env var DYNAMODB_TABLE

outputs.name: aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.arn
outputs.description: Table ARN, used in IAM policy to scope DynamoDB permissions

blocks.name: aws_lambda_function.api

description.high: Packages and deploys the API Lambda that handles HTTP requests from API Gateway. Gives it just enough permissions to read/write the tasks table and nothing more, plus the ability to send emails via SES.
description.tech: aws_lambda_function.api: runtime python3.12, handler handler.lambda_handler, filename from data.archive_file.lambda_zip (or pre-built deployment package), memory 256 MB, timeout 29s (API Gateway integration timeout ceiling). Environment block injects DYNAMODB_TABLE, SES_FROM_EMAIL, NOTIFY_EMAIL, AWS_SES_REGION. Shares aws_iam_role.lambda_exec which has: AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole managed policy attachment; inline policy allowing dynamodb:PutItem and dynamodb:Scan scoped to aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.arn and its GSI ARN; inline policy allowing ses:SendEmail on '*'. aws_lambda_function_url provides a direct HTTPS URL as a lightweight alternative to API Gateway for testing.

inputs.name: var.ses_from_email
inputs.description: SES sender address injected as SES_FROM_EMAIL env var

inputs.name: var.notify_email
inputs.description: Digest recipient injected as NOTIFY_EMAIL env var

inputs.name: var.aws_region
inputs.description: Region injected as AWS_SES_REGION env var

inputs.name: aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.name
inputs.description: Table name injected as DYNAMODB_TABLE env var

inputs.name: aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.arn
inputs.description: Table ARN used to scope IAM policy

outputs.name: aws_lambda_function.api.arn
outputs.description: Lambda ARN referenced by API Gateway integration and lambda permission

outputs.name: aws_lambda_function.api.invoke_arn
outputs.description: Invoke ARN used by aws_apigatewayv2_integration

outputs.name: aws_lambda_function_url.api.function_url
outputs.description: Direct HTTPS function URL for testing without API Gateway

blocks.name: aws_lambda_function.notify

description.high: Deploys the scheduled Lambda that checks for overdue tasks and sends the daily digest email. An EventBridge Scheduler rule fires it every morning at 08:00 UTC with a 5-minute flexible window to smooth AWS capacity.
description.tech: aws_lambda_function.notify: same runtime (python3.12) and deployment zip as api lambda, handler handler.notify_handler, memory 128 MB (no HTTP overhead), timeout 60s (accommodates DynamoDB full-table scan + SES call). Shares aws_iam_role.lambda_exec. aws_scheduler_schedule uses cron(0 8 * * ? *) in UTC, flexible_time_window of 5 minutes, and a target block pointing at the Lambda ARN with a role that has lambda:InvokeFunction permission. aws_iam_role.scheduler_exec is a separate role for EventBridge Scheduler to assume (scheduler.amazonaws.com principal).

inputs.name: aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.name
inputs.description: Table name injected as DYNAMODB_TABLE env var

inputs.name: var.ses_from_email
inputs.description: SES sender address injected as SES_FROM_EMAIL env var

inputs.name: var.notify_email
inputs.description: Digest recipient injected as NOTIFY_EMAIL env var

inputs.name: var.aws_region
inputs.description: Region injected as AWS_SES_REGION env var

outputs.name: aws_lambda_function.notify.arn
outputs.description: Lambda ARN targeted by the EventBridge Scheduler schedule

outputs.name: aws_scheduler_schedule.daily_digest
outputs.description: EventBridge Scheduler schedule that invokes notify Lambda daily at 08:00 UTC

blocks.name: aws_apigatewayv2_api.taskflow

description.high: Sets up the public HTTP API that routes GET and POST requests on the /tasks path to the API Lambda, and outputs the URL you call from a browser or mobile app.
description.tech: aws_apigatewayv2_api (HTTP API, protocol_type HTTP) with CORS configuration allowing all origins for development (tighten for production). Two aws_apigatewayv2_route resources ('GET /tasks', 'POST /tasks') backed by a single aws_apigatewayv2_integration of type AWS_PROXY with payload_format_version 2.0. aws_lambda_permission grants apigateway.amazonaws.com the lambda:InvokeFunction right on the api Lambda, scoped to the specific API ARN. aws_apigatewayv2_stage '$default' with auto_deploy=true and access logging to a CloudWatch log group. Output: invoke_url.

inputs.name: aws_lambda_function.api.invoke_arn
inputs.description: Lambda invoke ARN used in API Gateway integration

inputs.name: aws_lambda_function.api.arn
inputs.description: Lambda ARN scoped in aws_lambda_permission

outputs.name: api_invoke_url
outputs.description: Public HTTPS base URL for the TaskFlow API, e.g. https://<id>.execute-api.<region>.amazonaws.com

blocks.name: aws_ses_email_identity.sender

description.high: Registers the sender email address with Amazon SES so AWS will allow email to be sent from it. AWS sends a verification link to that address — the owner must click it before any emails can go out. Note: new AWS accounts are in the SES sandbox, which means you can only send to verified addresses; request production access in the SES console when ready.
description.tech: aws_ses_email_identity resource verifying var.ses_from_email in the deployment region. SES sandbox restrictions apply to new accounts: both sender and recipient must be individually verified, and daily sending quotas are capped. To send to arbitrary recipients, submit a 'Request production access' form in the SES console. The output exposes the DKIM tokens for optional Route 53 DNS record creation (not automated here). The resource is idempotent — re-applying re-sends the verification email if the address is not yet verified.

inputs.name: var.ses_from_email
inputs.description: Email address to register and verify with SES as the sender identity

outputs.name: aws_ses_email_identity.sender.arn
outputs.description: ARN of the verified SES email identity

outputs.name: ses_identity_arn
outputs.description: Output: ARN of the SES sender identity for reference in monitoring/alerting

blocks.code:
# ses.tf
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# SES sender email identity verification.
#
# IMPORTANT — SES Sandbox:
#   New AWS accounts are placed in the SES sandbox automatically.
#   In sandbox mode:
#     - Only VERIFIED email addresses can receive mail.
#     - Daily send quota is 200 emails, 1 email/second.
#   To send to arbitrary recipients, request production access:
#   https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ses/latest/dg/request-production-access.html
#
# After applying, AWS sends a verification email to var.ses_from_email.
# The owner MUST click the link before Lambda can send via this identity.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------

resource "aws_ses_email_identity" "sender" {
  email = var.ses_from_email
}

output "ses_identity_arn" {
  description = "ARN of the SES sender email identity."
  value       = aws_ses_email_identity.sender.arn
}

links.label: hydrates via from_row
links.description: Inside find(), TaskRepository calls Task.from_row(item) for every item dict returned by DynamoDB table.scan(), converting raw DynamoDB item dicts into Task dataclass instances.

dataStructure.name: DynamoDB item dict
dataStructure.format: Plain Python dict passed to Task.from_row()

fields.name: id
fields.description: UUID hex string assigned at save time

fields.name: title
fields.description: Task title

fields.name: due_date
fields.description: ISO-8601 datetime string; parsed by datetime.fromisoformat inside from_row()

fields.name: priority
fields.description: Priority level 1–3; stored as DynamoDB Number, cast to int in from_row()

fields.name: status
fields.description: Task status string; e.g. 'pending'

links.label: provides list[Task]
links.description: TaskRepository.find(status='pending') produces the full list[Task] consumed by notify_handler, which then filters it to overdue tasks (due_date < utcnow()) and passes the resulting overdue list to EmailNotifier.send_overdue_digest(). The notifier accesses task.title and task.due_date from each Task instance to build the digest body.

dataStructure.name: list[Task]
dataStructure.format: Python list of Task dataclass instances

fields.name: id
fields.description: Task UUID hex primary key

fields.name: title
fields.description: Task title; rendered in the email body as '- {title} (due {due_date})'

fields.name: due_date
fields.description: Task due date; compared against utcnow() in notify_handler and formatted as %Y-%m-%d in the email body

fields.name: priority
fields.description: Priority level; present on the object but not used by EmailNotifier

fields.name: status
fields.description: Task status; filtered to 'pending' by find() before overdue check

links.label: send_overdue_digest
links.description: notify_handler filters the pending task list to overdue items and passes them to EmailNotifier.send_overdue_digest(overdue, to_email), which delivers the digest via Amazon SES.

fields.name: overdue
fields.description: Tasks with due_date < utcnow() and status 'pending'

fields.name: to
fields.description: Recipient email address from NOTIFY_EMAIL env var

links.label: find / save
links.description: lambda_handler instantiates TaskRepository and calls either find(status) for GET /tasks or save(task) for POST /tasks, delegating all DynamoDB persistence to the repository.

fields.name: task
fields.description: Task dataclass passed to save() on POST

fields.name: status
fields.description: Optional status string passed to find() on GET

links.label: routes HTTP → Lambda
links.description: aws_apigatewayv2_integration wires the HTTP API's GET /tasks and POST /tasks routes to aws_lambda_function.api via an AWS_PROXY integration. aws_lambda_permission grants API Gateway the right to invoke the function.

fields.name: integration_uri
fields.description: aws_lambda_function.api.invoke_arn used in aws_apigatewayv2_integration

fields.name: source_arn
fields.description: aws_apigatewayv2_api.taskflow.execution_arn used to scope aws_lambda_permission

links.label: reads tasks table
links.description: aws_lambda_function.notify receives aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.name as the DYNAMODB_TABLE environment variable and is granted dynamodb:Scan on the table ARN via the shared IAM inline policy.

fields.name: DYNAMODB_TABLE
fields.description: aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.name injected as Lambda env var

fields.name: iam_resource_arn
fields.description: aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.arn used in IAM policy resource block

links.label: reads/writes tasks table
links.description: aws_lambda_function.api receives aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.name as the DYNAMODB_TABLE environment variable and is granted dynamodb:PutItem and dynamodb:Scan on the table ARN via the shared IAM inline policy.

fields.name: DYNAMODB_TABLE
fields.description: aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.name injected as Lambda env var

fields.name: iam_resource_arn
fields.description: aws_dynamodb_table.tasks.arn used in IAM policy resource block for PutItem and Scan

links.label: sends via verified identity
links.description: aws_lambda_function.api is granted ses:SendEmail on '*' via the shared IAM inline policy and uses the SES_FROM_EMAIL env var matching the verified aws_ses_email_identity.sender address.

fields.name: SES_FROM_EMAIL
fields.description: var.ses_from_email injected as Lambda env var; must match the verified SES identity

fields.name: ses_identity_email
fields.description: aws_ses_email_identity.sender.email — the verified sender address

links.label: sends via verified identity
links.description: aws_lambda_function.notify is granted ses:SendEmail on '*' via the shared IAM inline policy and uses the SES_FROM_EMAIL env var matching the verified aws_ses_email_identity.sender address.

fields.name: SES_FROM_EMAIL
fields.description: var.ses_from_email injected as Lambda env var; must match the verified SES identity

fields.name: ses_identity_email
fields.description: aws_ses_email_identity.sender.email — the verified sender address
